item.title:
I'd like to tell you about a personal silly weekend project.
Because I have love for the computing of the 90s, I built Relic, a tiny coding agent that runs on ancient devices. It fits on a floppy, ne
item.description:
I'd like to tell you about a personal silly weekend project.
Because I have love for the computing of the 90s, I built Relic, a tiny coding agent that runs on ancient devices. It fits on a floppy, needs 4MB of memory, and handles systems that were made before we got HTTPS. It's made with the same s

item.title:
Also out today: You can now directly configure the effort level and adaptive thinking in Code (/effort) and Cowork!
Effort allows you to tune Claude's intelligence vs token spend, trading off capabil
item.description:
Also out today: You can now directly configure the effort level and adaptive thinking in Code (/effort) and Cowork!
Effort allows you to tune Claude's intelligence vs token spend, trading off capability for faster speed and lower costs.
Opus 4.8 respects effort levels strictly, especially at the l

item.title:
Opus 4.8 is out! It's a nice little step up for some of your most demanding work, whether that's in Cowork or Code.
It's our strongest coding model yet. In my own work, I've found it to have excellen
item.description:
Opus 4.8 is out! It's a nice little step up for some of your most demanding work, whether that's in Cowork or Code.
It's our strongest coding model yet. In my own work, I've found it to have excellent judgement, both in how much work it should do and how it should react to my steering.
It's also g

item.title: I've moved into a somewhat older house so I'm having tons of little engineering problems in the REAL PHYSICAL WORLD but I keep arming myself with illustrated instruction manuals for my specific proble
item.description:
I've moved into a somewhat older house so I'm having tons of little engineering problems in the REAL PHYSICAL WORLD but I keep arming myself with illustrated instruction manuals for my specific problems and I feel unstoppable
I'm vibeplumbing https://t.co/B2wuFINtO7

item.title:
I'm moving and found a fun little use for Claude Cowork: Hand it a floorplan and ask it create a 3D planner. The attached demo (not my real place) was a one-shot.
I also had it find all the email rec
item.description:
I'm moving and found a fun little use for Claude Cowork: Hand it a floorplan and ask it create a 3D planner. The attached demo (not my real place) was a one-shot.
I also had it find all the email receipts for furniture I got over the last few years and put in matching models.
Yesterday, I asked it

item.title:
RT @ClaudeDevs: Usage limits are up, effective today we're:
1) Doubling Claude Code's 5-hour limits for Pro, Max, Team and seat-based Ent…
item.description:
RT @ClaudeDevs: Usage limits are up, effective today we're:
1) Doubling Claude Code's 5-hour limits for Pro, Max, Team and seat-based Ent…

item.title:
AI has broken my mental model for time estimates. I used to lean on Pareto's 80/20: first 80% of the work takes 20% of the time.
I think the underlying principle is still true, but both ratio and cu
item.description:
AI has broken my mental model for time estimates. I used to lean on Pareto's 80/20: first 80% of the work takes 20% of the time.
I think the underlying principle is still true, but both ratio and curve have moved. Getting to "basically works" is nearly instant, sweating every single last detail st
